shared_properties: The overlay covers the full screen and remains non-collidable like other world layers.
The center play zone stays open so core gameplay readability remains intact.
The asset relies on authored color rather than procedural masking for deterministic capture output.
differences: This overlay introduces the late-stage red warning treatment tied to the 2000px depth trigger.
Corner pooling is stronger than the side glow to make the climax phase feel more urgent.
The center remains clear so the player leaf, branch gates, and judgement text retain priority.

context.usage: Full-screen edge overlay that appears during the final high-BPM danger phase.

shape.silhouette: Soft red edge vignette with darker corner pooling and an open center play zone.

acceptance_criteria: PNG canvas is exactly 1280x720px with a center zone of at least 640x360px kept largely transparent.
Corner pooling uses darker reds than the edge glow so the warning feels directional and intense.
No edge treatment crosses over the top HUD anchors or the central rhythm marker track.

reference_images.label: leaf_beat_gdd
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the core loop, timing windows, obstacle pacing, UI layout, and Bevy target constraints.

reference_images.label: leaf_beat_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the sepia autumn atmosphere, tree silhouettes, and warm color layering.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteBundle overlay entity with opacity driven by depth-triggered gameplay timers.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load world/world_danger_vignette_overlay_1280x720.png during boot and fade its alpha from 0 to the authored target when depth crosses 2000px.
runtime_notes: Author the red edge treatment directly in the texture while gameplay code only controls overall opacity.
Keep the middle 640x360px area nearly transparent so rhythm notes and obstacles stay readable in the climax phase.
Render this overlay above world layers and below the judgement popup so warning pressure does not hide scoring feedback.
